sources.label: Globe and Mail / TipRanks - VAT Group hit by Q1 supply disruptions but reaffirms strong 2026 outlook
Morningstar - VAT Group AG (VACN) quote and fair value (CHF 515, coverage initiated Jun 1, 2026)
Investing.com - Goldman Sachs downgrades VAT Group to Neutral on valuation concerns
MarketScreener - VAT Group analyst consensus and price targets

verdicts.rankRationale: No material company-specific news since June 6 and today's -1.2% is noise; yesterday's hold already reconciled the +40% YTD re-rating. Small negative drift because the stock now trades above the ~CHF 607 consensus target and well above Morningstar's CHF 515 fair value, so residual alpha keeps compressing without new evidence.
verdicts.bull: 70%+ share in semiconductor vacuum valves with 80%+ in advanced control valves is a genuine bottleneck position; Q1 book-to-bill of ~1.6x and a reaffirmed record-2026 outlook show the leading-edge ramp is real and order momentum is accelerating.
verdicts.neutral: Strategically vital but fully discovered: 16-analyst coverage, Buy/Hold-split consensus, and a price target roughly at the current price mean the bottleneck status is already in the multiple. The thesis itself flags that quality valuation and semicap discovery cap alpha.
verdicts.bear: Goldman's Neutral downgrade cites a 121% EV/EBIT premium to multi-industry peers versus an 82% historical median; Q1 already showed Middle East supply-chain disruptions pushing revenue below guidance, and any semicap order digestion would hit a stock priced for a flawless ramp.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2/H1 2026 results confirming the record order intake converts to revenue, plus continued advanced-node and China semicap capex strength.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation de-rating from a 121% EV/EBIT peer premium if semicap orders digest or supply disruptions persist into H2.
